title: PD-L1 Low Driver-Negative NSCLC Opportunity
population: Driver-negative, non-squamous, PD-L1 low or negative
geography: Italy or EU, to be confirmed for access analysis
strategic_hypothesis: A biomarker-informed combination strategy could improve durable disease control in a subgroup where broad chemo-immunotherapy benefit is clinically variable and real-world testing gaps may affect optimal treatment selection.
unmet_need: Patients with driver-negative, PD-L1 low or negative metastatic NSCLC often have limited durable benefit from current broad treatment approaches, and implementation gaps in molecular testing can further weaken patient selection.

evidence.claim: PFS is commonly used as a primary endpoint in modern metastatic NSCLC trials, while OS remains the strongest value signal for practice-changing evidence.
evidence.source: Replace with current trial publications and guidelines before use
evidence.caveat: This example file is a scaffold and does not substitute for current evidence review.

evidence.claim: PD-L1 low or negative, driver-negative disease is a clinically important segment for unmet-need assessment.
evidence.source: Replace with current trial subgroup evidence, RWE, and guidelines
evidence.caveat: Requires up-to-date extraction of subgroup outcomes and guideline positioning.

trial_concept.intervention: Investigational differentiated combination added to platinum doublet backbone
trial_concept.comparator: Current guideline-concordant chemo-immunotherapy standard of care
secondary_endpoints: time to treatment discontinuation in pragmatic follow-up
trial_concept.biomarker_strategy: Baseline PD-L1 TPS, broad NGS, immune-resistance exploratory panel, and tissue or liquid biopsy adequacy metrics
trial_concept.rwe_component: Prospective pragmatic extension tracking testing completion, treatment duration, TTD, and post-progression sequence

swot_bridge.strength_fit: Use if the product has a differentiated mechanism, tolerability, biomarker logic, or combination rationale.
swot_bridge.weakness_mitigation: Avoid overclaiming broad-population superiority by focusing on a clearly defined evidence gap.
swot_bridge.opportunity_capture: Define a high-need segment where current outcomes and testing implementation leave room for improvement.
swot_bridge.threat_response: Address competitor congestion by anchoring differentiation to biomarker selection and pragmatic value evidence.

stakeholder_narratives.kol: The concept asks whether a biologically focused approach can clarify benefit in a subgroup where broad treatment strategies leave important uncertainty.
stakeholder_narratives.hcp: The study targets a real treatment decision point and would help refine which patients need more than the current default approach.
stakeholder_narratives.payer: The design could generate comparative and pragmatic evidence in a defined population, reducing uncertainty relevant to reimbursement and pathway decisions.
stakeholder_narratives.patient: The concept focuses on patients who still need longer disease control, tolerable treatment, and reliable access to appropriate testing.

elevator_pitch: A major gap in metastatic NSCLC is durable benefit for driver-negative, PD-L1 low or negative patients. The opportunity is to test a biomarker-informed combination against current standard care while measuring survival, safety, quality of life, and real-world treatment persistence. The design creates both clinical and access-relevant evidence by embedding testing quality and pragmatic follow-up. This matters now because differentiation in crowded lung cancer settings will depend on proving value in the patients least well served by broad strategies.
